title: Adaptive vent for knee airbag
abstract: A knee airbag (KAB) can be constructed with an adaptive vent for reducing internal cushion pressure when the deployment path is obstructed. Obstruction may occur, for instance, by an out-of-position occupant, or when the occupant's legs are in close proximity to the deploying KAB. By reducing the cushion pressure through adaptive venting, a potential for injury due to the deploying KAB can be reduced. External straps can be used to close the vents when no obstruction is encountered. With an adaptive KAB vent design, a direct contact between the occupant and the KAB adaptive vent straps can determine an amount of venting. In addition, the straps may be designed so that they are adaptive (tapered, positioned on an angle, or using different spacing) to account for different occupant sizes/knee spacing/knee contact locations.
claims:
Claims 1 . An airbag assembly comprising: an airbag housing that is configured to be mounted in a vehicle; an inflator configured to provide inflation gas during deployment; an airbag cushion coupled to the airbag housing and comprising a sidewall, the sidewall comprising a first aperture, the airbag cushion configured to receive the inflation gas from the inflator to expand the airbag cushion during deployment; a first strap external to the airbag cushion and attached to the airbag cushion at a first position and a second position, the first strap configured to be drawn taut between the first position and the second position and over the first aperture to obstruct the first aperture. 2. The airbag assembly of claim 1 , wherein the first strap is further configured to remain in a slack state with lack of tension to at least partially uncover the first aperture in response to contact with an occupant in an out-of-position condition. 3. The airbag assembly of claim 1 , further comprising: a second aperture formed in the airbag cushion; a second strap, attached to the airbag cushion at a third position and a fourth position, the second strap configured to be drawn taut between the third position and the fourth position and over the second aperture to obstruct the second aperture, wherein the second strap is further configured to remain in a slack state with lack of tension to at least partially uncover the second aperture in response to an occupant in an out-of-position condition. 4. The airbag assembly of claim 3, wherein the second aperture is formed in the sidewall. 5. The airbag assembly of claim 3, wherein the first strap and the second strap further comprise strap apertures configured to at least partially align with the first or second apertures in response to the occupant in the out-of-position condition. 6. The airbag assembly of claim 1 , wherein the first strap further comprises a strap aperture configured to at least partially align with the first aperture in response to contact with an occupant in an out-of-position condition. 7. The airbag assembly of claim 1 , wherein the first strap further comprises a plurality of strap apertures with varied areas configured to at least 

 partially align with the first aperture in response to an occupant in an out-of-position condition. 8. The airbag assembly of claim 1 , wherein the first strap is a panel configured to cover a plurality of apertures in the airbag cushion. 9. The airbag assembly of claim 8, wherein the panel includes at least one seam between two apertures in the airbag cushion. 10. The airbag assembly of claim 8, wherein the panel extends from a front of the airbag cushion to a back of the airbag cushion. 1 1 . The airbag assembly of claim 8, wherein the plurality of apertures include at least two apertures with different areas. 12. The airbag assembly of claim 1 , wherein the first strap is attached at the first position by a seam. 13. The airbag assembly of claim 12, wherein the seam is a shared airbag cushion seam that attaches the sidewall to another sidewall of the airbag cushion to form a chamber. 14. The airbag assembly of claim 1 , further comprising a strap guide coupled to the airbag cushion and receiving the first strap between a body of the guide and the airbag cushion. 15. An inflatable knee airbag configured to be positioned in a vehicle forward of an occupant position and at a bottom portion of one of an instrument panel and a seat back, wherein the inflatable knee airbag comprises: a plurality of panels defining an inflatable chamber, at least one panel comprising a vent, the inflatable chamber to receive inflation gas provided by an inflator; a strap external to the chamber and coupled to the inflatable chamber, the strap configured to: obstruct the vent when there is a pressure of the strap pushing against the vent; and at least partially uncover an opening of the vent when there is a lack of pressure of the strap against the vent opening. 16. The inflatable knee airbag of claim 15, wherein the pressure of the strap being pushed against the vent opening is to be caused by a large or tall occupant. 17. The inflatable knee airbag of claim 15, wherein the lack of pressure of the strap against the vent opening results from lack of contact with a small or short- statured occupant. 18. The inflatable knee airbag of claim 15, wherein the strap extends over a top portion of the inflatable chamber. 19. The inflatable knee airbag of claim 18, wherein a first end of the strap is attached to a front of the inflatable chamber and a second end of the strap is attached to a back of the inflatable chamber. 20. The inflatable knee airbag of claim 15, wherein the strap is a panel. 21 . The inflatable knee airbag of claim 20, wherein the strap is configured to cover a plurality of vents. 22. An out-of-position strap valve for an airbag, the valve comprising: an aperture formed within a sidewall of an airbag; and a strap extending across the aperture, external to the sidewall and tethered to a first position and a second position on the sidewall of the airbag, the strap configured to obstruct the aperture in response to tension in the strap due to the strap being pulled taut between the first position and the second position and over the valve aperture. 23. The strap valve of claim 22, wherein the strap is further configured to at least partially uncover an opening of the valve aperture as a result of a lack of tension in the strap. 24. The strap valve of claim 23, wherein the lack of tension occurs in response to contact with an occupant in an out-of-position condition. 25. The strap valve of claim 24, wherein the lack of tension occurs as the airbag is inflated with a lack of contact with a small or short-statured occupant. 26. The strap valve of claim 22, wherein the tension occurs as the airbag is inflated to receive an occupant in an in-position condition. 27. The strap valve of claim 22, wherein the tension occurs in response to contact with an occupant.
